Fern PolrrrcAL PnecrrcEs C oMMrssroN
-ll8 J Street o Suire 620 o Sacramento. CA 9-5gl.l_2-129
(916) 322--s660 o Fax (916) 322-0U86
September 16,2009
Dianna J. Koelzer
2040 French Knoll
Shingle Springs, CA 95682
Re
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-09-192
Dear Ms. Koelzer:
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the revolving door
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 Pleise note this letter is based on the
facts presented. The Fair Political Practices Commission ("the Commission,,) does not act
as a finder offact when it renders assistance. Because you have asked for general
intbrmation, we are providing informal assisrance.2 (li re Oglesby (1975) 1 FppC Ops.
7 t.)
Please bear in mind also that our advice is based solely on the provisions of the
Act- We offer no opinion on the application, if any, of such provisioni as Government
Code Section 1090, or other post-government employment liws such as public Contract
Code Section l04ll.
QUESTTON
What limitations does the Act impose on you in your capacity as a former state
employee now that you work for a firm that consults wiih Californiastate agencies for
project management?
I The Political Refbrm Act is contained in Government Code Sections g 1000 through 9 I 014. All
statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulationi of the Fair
PoliticalPractices Commission are contained in Sections l8l l0 rhrough lgggT of Title 2 of rhe california
Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2. Division 6 of the California Code of
Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.
r Informal assistance does not provide the requestur with the immunity provided by an opinion or
fbrmal written advice. lSection 83r r4: regulation 1g319(c)(3), copy enclosedj.


File No. l-09-192
Page No. 2
CONCLUSION
You are subject to the provisions of the Act's "one-year ban" and "permanent"
ban as described below. You may not communicate with your former empl,oyer, for
compensation, on behalf of another for a period on one-year after you leave state service
if that contact is made for the purpose of influencing administrative or legislative actions
or any discretionary act involving the issuance, amendment, awarding, or revocation of a
permit, license, grant, or contract, or the sale or purchase of goods or property.
Additionally, you are prohibited from "switching sides" and participating, for
compensation, in any specific proceeding involving the State of California or assisting
others in the proceeding if the proceeding is one in which you participated while
employed by the state.
FACTS
You are a former employee of the Califomia Department of Child Support
Services, Health and Human Services agency. In that capacity, you were a project
Director of the California Child Support Automation Project ("CCSAS"), a deslgnated
position. Your project concerned implementing a new State-wide system for processing
child support. You reported to the director of the program and coordinated with
representatives from the Franchise Tax Board. You retired from this position on
February 28,2009. You also worked in designated positions at the California Lottery
(2000-2004) and the Employment Development Department (pre-2004).
You are now a partner for a firm called Kenera Consulting, Inc. ("Kenera"), an IT
consulting firm that specializes in project management, strategic planning, data
conversion, and integration. Kenera plans to bid on various State IT projects. your
duties will be primarily business development, including meeting with prospective
clients, drafting bids for request for proposals, and finalizing statementi of work, and
"client work," which would depend on the types of services that the client requests,
including project management, change management, and strategic planning. buring our
telephone conversations, you stated that Kenera occasionally engages in a imeet and
greet" to introduce itself to potential clients, explain its services, and make itself available
for potential new business. You sometimes participate in these events.
ANALYSIS
Public officials are subject to two pertinent types of post-governmental
restrictions under the Act. A one-year "revolving door" restriction bars certain state
employees from communicating, for compensation, with their former agencies for the
purpose of influencing certain administrative or legislative action. (Section 87406,
Regulation 18746.1.) A permanent ban prohibits a former state employee from
"switching sides" to participate, for compensation, in a proceeding involving the State of
California if the proceeding is one in which the former state employee partiaipated while
employed by the state. (Sections 87401-87402, Regulation LB74L.l.)


File No. l-09-192
Page No. 3
One-Year Ban
Regulation 18746.L(b) outlines the circumstances when the prohibitions of the
one-year ban will apply. Under this regulation, an official covered by the one-year ban is
prohibited from making an appearance or communication if all of the following apply:
"(l) The official has left his or her state office or employment, which means
he or she has either permanently left state service or is on a leave of absence.
"(2) The appearance or communication is made within 12 months after
leaving state office or employment.
"(3) The public official is compensated, or promised compensation, for the
appearance or communication. However, a payment made for necessary
travel, meals, and accommodations received directly in connection with
voluntary services is not prohibited or limited by this section.
"(4) The appearance or corlmunication is made on behalf of any person as
an agent, attomey, or representative of that person. An appearance or
communication made by a public official solely to represent his or her
personal interests, as defined in2 Cal. code Regs., Section lg7oz.4,
subdivision (bxl), is not prohibited or limited by this section.
"(5) The appearance or communication is made for the purpose of
influencing, as defined in2 Cal. Code Regs. Section 19746.2, any
legislative or administrative action, or any discretionary act invoiving the
issuance, amendment, awarding, or revocation of a permit, license, giant
or contract, or the sale or purchase of goods or property.
"(A) Services performed to administer, implement, or fulfill the
requirements of an existing permit, license, grant, contract, or sale
agreement may be excluded from the prohibitions of this regulation,
provided the services do not involve the issuance, amendment, awarding,
or revocation of any of these actions or proceedings . . ..
"(6) The appearance or corlmunication is made before any officer or
employee of any of the following:
"(A) Any state administrative agency that the public official worked for or
represented during the 12 months before leaving state office or
employment....
"(B) Any state administrative agency which budget, personnel, and other
operations are spbject to the direction and control of any agency described
in subdivision (b)(6)(A) . . ..
"(c) Any state administrative agency subject to the direction and control
of the Governor, if the official was a designated employee of the


File No. l-09-192
Page No.4
Govern0r's office during the 12 months before leaving state office or
employment."
Commttnications Covered by the One-Year Ban
Communications restricted by the one-year ban include any formal or informal
appearance or oral or written communication made to influence legislative or
administrative action or any action on a proceeding. (Section 87406(dXl).) These
communications include, but are not limited to, conversing directly or by telephone,
corresponding by writing or e-mail, attending a meeting, and delivering or sending any
communication. (Regulation L8746.2(a).) A communication is considered to be for the
purpose of influencing legislative or administrative action "if it is made for the principal
purpose of supporting, promoting, inluencing, modifying, opposing, delaying, or
advancing the action or proceeding." 3 (Regulation t9746.2(i) -
certain communications are no, restricted under the one-year ban. A
communication is not subject to the one-year ban when the former official:
"(l) Participates as a panelist or formal speaker at a conference or similar
public event for educational purposes or to disseminate research and the
subject matter does not pertain to a specific action or proceeding;
"(2) Attends a general informational meeting, seminar, or similar event;
"(3) Requests information concerning any matter of pubric record; or
"(4) Communicates with the pre5s." (Regulation lB746.2(b)(I)-(4).)
Thus, the one-year ban would not prevent you from requesting information
generally available to the public about agency business from your former employer or
other state agencies. Nor would it prohibit you from attending informational meetings
regarding existing laws, regulations, or policies, so long as you do not attempt to
influence legislative or administrative actions by your former agency. Social
conversations with employees of your former agency that are not aimed at influencing its
administrative or legislative actions are not prohibited by the ban. (Tobias Advice Letter,
No. 4-96-089.) (Section 87a06(d)(1).)
Whether a particular meeting or conversation is for the purpose of influencing
administrative or legislative action or a specific proceeding (as defined by Regulation
I "Legislative action" is defined at Section 82037 to mean "the drafting, introduction,
consideration, modification, enactment or defeat of any bill, resolution, amendment, report, nomination or
other matter by the Legislature or by either house or any committee, subcommittee, joint or select
committee thereof, or by a member or employee of the Legislature acting in his offiiial capacity.
"Legislative action" also means the action of the Governor in approving or vetoing any bili." Section
82002(a) provides that "'Administrative action' means the proposal, drafting, development, consideration,
amendment, enactment, or defeat by any state agency of any rule, regulation, or other action in any
ratemaking proceeding or ahy quasi-legislative proceeding, which shall include any proceeding governed
by chapter 3.5 (commencing with Secrion I 1340) of part I of Division 3 of ritle 2.


File No. I-09-192
Page No. 5
18746.2) depends on the facts of each case. For instance, if an ex-employee attends a
public meeting with many other persons, where there are many topics onihe agenda, it
may be reasonable to infer that the ex-employee's attendunc" i, not for the purpose of
influencing the agency's action. Conversely, where there is a small meetin! to discuss a
particular administrative or legislative action, or other specific action or pro"ceeding
involving the issuance, amendment, awarding, or revocation of a permit, license, grant, or
contract, or the sale,or purchase of goods or property (section g74b6(d)), it may more
readily be inferred that the former employee;s piesence at the meeting is intended to
influence agency action. (RamirezAdvice Letter, No. A-99-300.)a
Similarly, as noted above, Regulation 18746.1(b)(sXA) provides that performing
services to administer, implement, or fulfill the requirements of an existing p"..it,
license, grant, contract, or sale agreement are not considered upp"ur*."r ii
communications prohibited under Section 87406, so long as you do not influence other
administrative or legislative action, or the issuance, ameridment, awarding, or revocation of
a permit, license, grant, or contract, or the sale or purchase of goods o, p--p"rty.
Accordingly, for purposes of the one-year ban under Section B7406,the Act does
not prevent you from communicating with your former agency, but it limits your
communications as described above. The Act prohibits you fiom participating in ,.meet
and greet" events to the extent that you are attemptlng to influence a future coirtract.
Regarding current or future contracts that Kenera tras witfr the Department of Child
Support Services, the Act prohibits you from attempting to influence the decisions that
department makes, but the prohibition does not appiy tli" ,u*" prohibition to your firm or
business partner. That is, your partner may participate in the "meet and greet,; events and
may post bids under the company name or under hir name. (See, e.g., ItiihoroAdvice
Letter, I-09-047.)
The Permanent Ban on,,switching Sides,
The permanent ban prohibits a former state employee from "switching sides,, and
participating, for compensation, in any specifi" p.o"""dirg involving ttre stat? of
Califomia or assisting others in the proceeding if the procJeding is one in which the
Illry. state employee participated while empioyed by the state. (,See Sections g741l-
87 402; Regulation 187 41.1.)
The permanent ban is a lifetime ban applicable to any judicial, quasi-judicial, or
other proceeding in which you participated while employed ui u rtut.'ua-ini"trutiu.
official. "'Judicial, quasi-judicial or other proceeding' rn"un. any proceeding,
application, request for a ruling or other deierminatio-n, contract, claim, contriversy,
investigation, charge, accusation, arrest or other particular matter involving a specific
party or parties in any court or state administrative agency . . .." (Section S'Z+Ob1c).) An
official has "participated" in a proceeding if he or sh-e took part in the proceeding
"personally, and substantially through decision, approval, disapprovaf, formai *ritten
r Further infbrmation is provided in the enclosed fact sheet prepared by the Commission to address
common questions associated with post_employment restrictions.


File No. I-09-192
Page No. 6
recommendation' rendering advice on a substantial basis, investigation, or use of
confidential information . . ..', (Section g7400(d).)
The permanent ban does not apply to a "new" proceeding even in cases where the
new proceeding is related to or grows out of a prior prbceeding i-n which the official had
participated. A "new" proceeding nol subject to the permarert ban typically involves
different parties, subject matter, or circumstances that differ from those involved in
previous proceedings. (Rlsr Advice Letter, No. A_04_lg7; also see Donovan Advice
Letter, No. I-03-l19.) A "new" contract is one based on new consideration and new
terms, even if it involves the same parties. (FerberAdvice Letter, No. I_99_104;
Anderson Advice Letter, No. 4-98-159.) In addition, the application, draftinj and the
award of a contract, license, or approval is considered a procieding separate irom tt e
monitoring and perfonirance of the contract, license, o, upprorul. (Anderson, slrpra;
Blonien Advice Letter, No. 4-89-463.)
You explained that your position with the Department of Child Support Services
involved project management for the state's centraliied child suppon.orrifu,", system.
The project you describe does not appear to be a "proceeding" uri". the Act because it
does not appear to involve "a specific party or parties in any court or state administrative
agency" concerning an "application, request for a ruling or other determination, contract,
claim, controversy, investigation, charge, accusation, aiest or other particular matter
involving." However, because you have not provided enough facts we are unable to make
that determination. You may, however, coniider the infoniation we have provided to
determine whether your project at the Department of Child Support Services would more
appropriately be considered a "proceeding." If you need furth#assistance, please feel
free to write for additional advice ana proviae a complete factual description of the
project.
Additionally, you may not participate in proceedings in which you were involved
during your services with the california iottery and the Eirployment o"u"tof,,,rnt
Department, as discussed above.
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
lh-rJ'l*tt,(la*
By: Heather M. Rowan
Counsel, Legal Division
HMR jgl
Enclosure

File No. l-09-192 Page No. 2 CONCLUSION You are subject to the provisions of the Act's "one-year ban" and "permanent" ban as described below. You may not communicate with your former empl,oyer, for compensation, on behalf of another for a period on one-year after you leave state service if that contact is made for the purpose of influencing administrative or legislative actions or any discretionary act involving the issuance, amendment, awarding, or revocation of a permit, license, grant, or contract, or the sale or purchase of goods or property. Additionally, you are prohibited from "switching sides" and participating, for compensation, in any specific proceeding involving the State of California or assisting others in the proceeding if the proceeding is one in which you participated while employed by the state.
